texto: A person acquainted with the true principles of this science, who preserves his Dharma (virtue or religious merit), his Artha (worldly wealth) and his Kama (pleasure or sensual gratification), and who has regard to the customs of the people, is sure to obtain the mastery over his senses. In short, an intelligent and knowing person attending to Dharma and Artha and also to Kama, without becoming the slave of his passions, will obtain success in everything that he may do.
explicacao: Citação de Mallanaga Vātsyāyana sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “A person acquainted with the true principles of this science, who preserves his Dharma (virtue or…” articula sucesso, artha, dharma em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como termino e dharma.

frase_original: A person acquainted with the true principles of this science, who preserves his Dharma (virtue or religious merit), his Artha (worldly wealth) and his Kama (pleasure or sensual gratification), and who has regard to the customs of the people, is sure to obtain the mastery over his senses. In short, an intelligent and knowing person attending to Dharma and Artha and also to Kama, without becoming the slave of his passions, will obtain success in everything that he may do.

texto: IF a woman has manifested her love or desire, either by signs or by motions of the body,and is afterwards rarely or never seen anywhere, or if a woman is met for the first time,the man should get a go-between to approach her.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Mallanaga Vātsyāyana sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “IF a woman has manifested her love or desire, either by signs or by motions of the body,and is af…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, approach, desire, útil em momentos de desire e go-between.
